title: Africa Lifetime Education Icon Special Recognition Award
description: The Africa Lifetime Education Icon Special Recognition Award is the pinnacle of the NESA-Africa awards and the Santos Creations Educational Foundation. Envisioned as the Africa education advocacy Nobel award recognition, this prestigious accolade honors individuals from around the world who have dedicated their lives to advancing sustainable education for all in Africa, aligning with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4) - Quality Education.
subCategories/0/title: Africa Education Philanthropy Icon Of The Decade (2014-2024)
subCategories/0/description: Honors significant philanthropic contributions that have improved educational access and quality, advancing SDG 4's targets.
subCategories/0/nominees/0/achievement: Established the 'Dangote Foundation' in 1993, which has invested millions in educational causes across Africa.
subCategories/0/nominees/1/achievement: Founded the 'Mo Ibrahim Foundation' in 2006, supporting governance and leadership in Africa.
subCategories/0/nominees/2/achievement: Established the 'Higher Life Foundation' in 1996, providing scholarships to over 250,000 African students.
subCategories/0/nominees/3/achievement: Established the 'Rose of Sharon Foundation' in 2008, providing scholarships and educational support to widows and orphans.
subCategories/0/nominees/4/achievement: Founded the 'Motsepe Foundation' in 1999, focusing on education, scholarships, and school infrastructure in South Africa.
subCategories/0/nominees/5/achievement: Established the 'Tony Elumelu Foundation' in 2010, providing scholarships and promoting entrepreneurship education across Africa.
subCategories/0/nominees/6/achievement: Founded the 'Isabel dos Santos Foundation' in 2014, which has funded educational projects and scholarships in Angola.
subCategories/0/nominees/7/achievement: Founded the 'Sawiris Foundation for Social Development' in 2001, supporting higher education and scholarships in Egypt.
subCategories/0/nominees/8/achievement: Founded the 'Despite Foundation,' supporting education through scholarships and school building projects in Ghana.
subCategories/0/nominees/9/achievement: Co-founded the 'Capernaum Trust' in 1996, providing scholarships and educational support to orphans and vulnerable children.
subCategories/1/title: Literary And New Curriculum Advocate Africa Education Icon Of The Decade (2014-2024)
subCategories/1/description: Honors significant contributions to literature and curriculum development in African education.
subCategories/1/nominees/0/achievement: Founded the 'Ngugi wa Thiong'o Foundation' in 1980, promoting indigenous languages in education in Kenya.
subCategories/1/nominees/1/achievement: Founded the 'Chinua Achebe Foundation' in 1990, promoting African literature and supporting literary education (posthumously).
subCategories/1/nominees/2/achievement: Established the 'Mariama Ba School' in 1979, a beacon of girls' education in Senegal.
subCategories/1/nominees/3/achievement: Dedication to improving rural education, particularly for girls, demonstrating a significant impact on her community.
subCategories/1/nominees/4/achievement: Dedication to improving rural education, particularly for girls, demonstrating a significant impact on her community.
subCategories/1/nominees/5/achievement: Founded the 'Farafina Trust' in 2008, promoting literary education and supporting young writers in Nigeria.
subCategories/1/nominees/6/achievement: Established the 'Wole Soyinka Centre for Investigative Journalism' in 2004, promoting media literacy and education in Nigeria.
subCategories/1/nominees/7/achievement: Founded the 'Mbaasem Foundation' in 2000, supporting the development of African women writers and promoting literacy in Ghana.
subCategories/1/nominees/8/achievement: Co-founded the 'African Speculative Fiction Society' in 2016, promoting African literature in science fiction and fantasy.
subCategories/1/nominees/9/achievement: Founded the 'Teju Cole Literary Project' in 2010, promoting literature and creative writing in Nigeria.
subCategories/2/title: Africa Technical Educator Icon Of The Decade (2014-2024)
subCategories/2/description: Recognizes outstanding contributions to technical education in Africa.
subCategories/2/nominees/0/achievement: Founder of 'Ashesi University' in 2002, which emphasizes leadership, ethics, and entrepreneurship in its technical and liberal arts education.
subCategories/2/nominees/1/achievement: Co-founder of 'LEAP Africa' in 2002, which focuses on leadership and life skills training for young people.
subCategories/2/nominees/2/achievement: Founder of 'African Leadership University' in 2015, a network of tertiary institutions focused on developing Africa's next generation of leaders.
subCategories/2/nominees/3/achievement: Co-founder of 'BudgIT' in 2011, which uses data to drive education in governance and public policy.
subCategories/2/nominees/4/achievement: Founder of 'mPedigree' in 2007, an organization that uses mobile technology to enhance healthcare and education services.
subCategories/2/nominees/5/achievement: Founder of 'AppsTech' in 1999, which provides enterprise application solutions and has supported IT education in Africa.
subCategories/2/nominees/6/achievement: Co-founder of 'AkiraChix' in 2010, which empowers young women in Kenya through technical training in software development.
subCategories/2/nominees/7/achievement: Co-founder of 'Lidya' in 2016, a digital financial services platform that provides financial literacy and resources to small businesses in Africa.
subCategories/2/nominees/8/achievement: Co-founder of 'Flutterwave' in 2016, supporting fintech education and training across Africa.
subCategories/2/nominees/9/achievement: Founder of 'Moringa School' in 2014, a Nairobi-based technology and coding bootcamp training the next generation of software developers.

title: Diaspora Association Educational Impact in Africa
description: This Award recognizes and honors the significant contributions made by the Nigerian diaspora towards achieving 'Education for All' in Nigeria. This award, set for the 2023 cycle, aims to celebrate diaspora individuals, groups, or organizations that have made a substantial impact through skills transfer, corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives, advocacy, and other educational support back home in Nigeria.
subCategories/0/description: This category celebrates diaspora organizations that have created significant educational infrastructure, advancing the quality of education across Africa.
subCategories/0/nominees/0/achievement: Constructed a science and technology center in Osun State, Nigeria, fully equipped with modern laboratories and classrooms.
subCategories/0/nominees/1/achievement: Built a community library and learning center in Kumasi, Ghana, with a focus on providing access to books and digital resources.
subCategories/0/nominees/2/achievement: Constructed three primary schools in rural areas of Kenya, including the provision of solar power and internet access.
subCategories/0/nominees/3/achievement: Renovated dilapidated schools in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, including upgrading classrooms, sanitation facilities, and playgrounds.
subCategories/0/nominees/4/achievement: Funded the construction of a multi-purpose education and sports complex in Soweto, South Africa. Provided a safe space for learning and recreation.
subCategories/0/nominees/5/achievement: Built a secondary school in Gulu, Uganda, focusing on regions recovering from conflict. Provided quality education to over 500 students.
subCategories/0/nominees/6/achievement: Led the refurbishment of schools in Matabeleland, Zimbabwe, including the installation of computer labs and solar power systems.
subCategories/0/nominees/7/achievement: Funded the construction of a vocational training center in Dakar, Senegal, focusing on trades such as carpentry, plumbing, and electrical work.
subCategories/0/nominees/8/achievement: Established digital learning hubs in rural Tanzania, providing internet access and e-learning tools to underserved communities.
subCategories/0/nominees/9/achievement: Built a school complex in Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of the Congo, including classrooms, a library, and a computer lab.
subCategories/1/title: The Best Diaspora-Led Educational Program Innovation
subCategories/1/description: This category recognizes innovative educational programs initiated by diaspora organizations that have significantly impacted learning outcomes in Africa.
subCategories/1/nominees/0/achievement: Developed an e-learning platform that offers free access to STEM courses for students across Africa. Trained 5,000 teachers across the continent.
subCategories/1/nominees/1/achievement: Reached over 10,000 students in multiple countries, significantly improving their understanding and performance in science subjects.
subCategories/1/nominees/2/achievement: Designed an after-school leadership program that fosters entrepreneurial skills among African youth. Over 1,000 students have participated.
subCategories/1/nominees/3/achievement: Introduced a mentorship and investment program for young entrepreneurs in Nigeria, combining educational resources with financial support.
subCategories/1/nominees/4/achievement: Developed a distance learning program that connects Somali students in rural areas with diaspora educators via virtual classrooms.
subCategories/1/nominees/5/achievement: Launched a digital literacy program that provides Eritrean students with essential IT skills, including coding and digital communication.
subCategories/1/nominees/6/achievement: Established a bilingual education program that supports French and Arabic literacy in Moroccan schools using digital tools.
subCategories/1/nominees/7/achievement: Developed an IT skills training program focused on coding, web development, and digital marketing for Ghanaian youth.
subCategories/1/nominees/8/achievement: Introduced an environmental education program that teaches sustainable practices and conservation in Botswana's schools.
subCategories/1/nominees/9/achievement: Implemented an after-school tutoring and mentorship program in Liberia, focusing on literacy and numeracy skills. Improved academic performance for over 5,000 students.
subCategories/2/title: The Best Diaspora-Led Teacher Training And Support Initiative
subCategories/2/description: This category honors diaspora-led initiatives that have significantly improved teacher training and support across Africa.
subCategories/2/nominees/0/achievement: Delivered a teacher training program covering modern pedagogical methods, classroom management, and technology integration.
subCategories/2/nominees/1/achievement: Established a mentorship program connecting female educators in Africa with experienced teachers abroad. Empowered 2,000 women in education.
subCategories/2/nominees/2/achievement: Launched a continuous professional development program for Zimbabwean teachers, focusing on curriculum development and modern teaching methods.
subCategories/2/nominees/3/achievement: Developed an annual teacher training conference in Accra, Ghana, featuring workshops on best practices in education and technology integration.
subCategories/2/nominees/4/achievement: Implemented a peer-to-peer support network for Ugandan teachers, offering resources, mentorship, and collaborative professional development.
subCategories/2/nominees/5/achievement: Conducted teacher training workshops focusing on inclusive education and teaching students with special needs. Trained over 1,000 educators.
subCategories/2/nominees/6/achievement: Provided remote training and resources for teachers in Sierra Leone, focusing on post-conflict education and trauma-informed teaching practices.
subCategories/2/nominees/7/achievement: Established a teacher exchange program that brings Ethiopian educators to the United States for training in STEM education. Improved science and math instruction in over 100 Ethiopian schools.
subCategories/2/nominees/8/achievement: Developed a peace education curriculum and trained teachers in South Sudan on conflict resolution and peacebuilding. Trained over 500 educators.
subCategories/2/nominees/9/achievement: Developed a STEM-focused teacher training initiative, providing resources and training to Zambian teachers. Trained 1,500 teachers in innovative science and math instruction methods.
